plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and align with single name limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure entirely for capital preservation.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to boost income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLV to diversify into a defensive sector like healthcare.
This plan aims for a stable portfolio with minimal leverage and reduced concentration, holding a substantial cash buffer.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation and stability. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but still carries market risk, especially from broad index movements.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall broad index exposure slightly.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove all leveraged ETFs, reducing overall portfolio risk.
Trim QQQI to 15% to rebalance the income component while maintaining some exposure.
Increase CRWD to 15% to lean into a strong-performing AI/Software name.
Buy GOOGL at 10% to add another high-quality tech name with good scanner setup.
This plan balances growth with risk management, reducing leverage and diversifying into quality tech.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, aiming for risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and diversifies into quality tech, but remains exposed to market volatility and sector-specific risks.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to comply with single name concentration limits.
Trim QQQI to 10% to reallocate capital towards higher growth potential assets.
Increase SOXL to 10% to lean into leveraged semiconductor exposure, accepting higher risk.
Increase CRWD to 20% to press a strong-performing AI/Software name.
Increase SMH to 15% to further concentrate in the semiconductor sector.
Buy ANET at 10% to add a high risk/reward tech setup from the scanner.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It significantly increases concentration in tech/semis and leverages exposure, leading to much higher potential drawdowns and daily decay. Strict stop discipline is crucial to manage amplified losses.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits while maintaining broad market exposure.
Sell all SOXL, SPAL, SMH, and CRWD to eliminate leveraged ETFs and high-beta growth names.
Significantly increase QQQI to 40% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield, prioritizing low drawdown and reliable income.
It aims for cash-like income, but distributions are variable and NAV can still fall.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It drastically reduces leverage and high-beta assets, prioritizing income and low drawdown. However, distributions are variable, and NAV can still decline in market selloffs, it is not a savings account.
